name: Chainguard Libraries for Python
license: Proprietary (commercial SaaS); the open-source keyring auth backend (keyrings-chainguard-libraries) is Apache-2.0
cost: Commercial, quote-based. Licensed per language ecosystem by the size of your engineering org -- you pay only for developers who write/build/deploy with each licensed language (Python, Java, JavaScript). Subscription includes unlimited applications per ecosystem with no pull metering or rate limiting; volume/multi-year and multi-product-bundle discounts plus startup/SMB pricing are available. Free for all Chainguard Console users from Apr 9, 2026 through Jun 30, 2026, no commitment required.
language: Not publicly disclosed (the index service is closed-source; the chainctl CLI is Go, the keyring auth backend is Python)
repo: https://github.com/chainguard-dev/keyrings-chainguard-libraries (keyring auth helper only; the index service itself is closed-source)
perf_notes: No published throughput/latency/memory benchmarks (managed SaaS endpoint at libraries.cgr.dev). Vendor metrics are security-efficacy oriented: a Chainguard analysis of ~3,000 malicious PyPI packages found the overwhelming majority (~98%) would have been avoided by consuming Chainguard's rebuilt-from-source libraries; targets the 600k+ package PyPI ecosystem.
scale_notes: Multi-tenant managed SaaS on Chainguard's hardened cloud (libraries.cgr.dev); subscription includes unlimited applications per ecosystem with no pull metering/rate limiting. HA/scaling are vendor-managed and not user-configurable or documented; not self-hostable. The catalog is a curated rebuild of popular PyPI packages plus their full dependency trees, with a configurable cooldown-gated upstream fallback for packages not yet rebuilt rather than a full 1:1 PyPI mirror.
summary: A commercial SaaS Python package index (libraries.cgr.dev, GA Oct 2025) serving malware-resistant dependencies rebuilt entirely from source in Chainguard's SLSA-hardened Factory, dropped in as an authenticated upstream replacement for PyPI with Sigstore-signed SLSA provenance (served at a PEP 740 integrity endpoint), embedded SBOMs (PEP 770), and CVE remediation.
extras: Packages rebuilt entirely from source in Chainguard's SLSA-hardened 'Factory', so source-less malware (the bulk of analyzed PyPI malware samples) cannot survive the rebuild; install-time scripts and unverifiable sources are excluded

GA reached Oct 22, 2025 with CVE remediation: backported high/critical fixes shipped via a separate remediated index (libraries.cgr.dev/python-remediated/simple), versions tagged with a +cgr.N local identifier (e.g. flask 1.1.2+cgr.1) so they take resolver precedence; VEX advisories accompany fixes
Multiple index endpoints, all in PyPI repository format: base (/python/simple/), remediated (/python-remediated/simple/), and CUDA-specific indexes (cu126/cu128/cu129)

PEP 740 SLSA provenance is served at a PEP 740 integrity endpoint (https://libraries.cgr.dev/python/integrity/PACKAGE/VERSION/FILE/provenance) rather than inline in the HTML simple index; chainctl computes a SHA-512 digest locally and uses cosign to verify the Sigstore-signed bundle out-of-band
Signed SBOM ships with every package, embedded in the wheel per PEP 770 (*.dist-info/sboms/sbom.spdx.json, SPDX, crediting Chainguard, Inc.)

Auth via pull tokens minted by chainctl over Chainguard's OIDC identity platform (HTTP basic auth; default TTL 720h/30 days, max 8760h/365 days); the keyrings-chainguard-libraries backend (Apache-2.0) mints short-lived tokens and auto-injects creds for *.cgr.dev for pip and uv (keyring-provider)

Static-credential alternatives: pull-token username/password in the index URL or a .netrc entry for libraries.cgr.dev; credentials can come from CHAINGUARD_PYTHON_IDENTITY_ID / CHAINGUARD_PYTHON_TOKEN env vars (per-ecosystem variants exist for Java and JavaScript)
Per-ecosystem RBAC roles (e.g. libraries.python.pull_token_creator) and org-scoped entitlements managed in the Chainguard Console; SSO/OIDC via the Chainguard identity platform
Read-only consumption index: no twine upload, no private-package hosting, no trusted-publishing -- it replaces PyPI as an authenticated upstream, it does not host your own packages

Configurable cooldown-gated upstream fallback: requests for packages not yet rebuilt are gated, logged, and prioritized for a new from-source build (quarantine-style); docs also recommend configuring PyPI as a fallback in your own repository manager
Native-code wheels handled by rebuilding bundled OS libraries (e.g. OpenSSL) from source; multiple wheel variants per Python version / architecture (x86_64, aarch64) / glibc level
Part of a multi-ecosystem family: Chainguard Libraries is also offered for Java and JavaScript, licensed separately per ecosystem
Documented build tools are pip, uv, and Poetry (with .netrc as the cross-tool auth method); pdm/Pipfile/pylock.toml lockfile hashes are maintained via 'chainctl libraries update-hashes'
Managed via the Chainguard Console web UI (token creation, org/IAM, browsing)
